title: La Stradangelion
wikitext:
[]: La Stradangelion?             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Apr 15, 2011 6:07 am This is probably a leap, but I do think it's worth mentioning. Ever since I saw Federico Fellini's 1954 film La Strada , I've wondered if its finale served as inspiration for EOE's own conclusion. Anno seems fairly well versed in classic cinema, so the possibility exists. Still, I can find only the broadest of parallels between the films, even if this clip looks familiar. See what you think: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4FMhJ2A2IDQ More info: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/La_strada

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Apr 15, 2011 6:25 am As much as I love La Strada, I don't think it fits. Protagonist falls down on the beach and bewails the death of a character he hadn't seen in a while after getting drunk doesn't sound much like EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Fri Apr 15, 2011 6:34 am You're probably right, but as we know, what IS happening in EOE is an eternal debate. La Strada's ending concerns the protagonist guilt over abandoning his female companion (among other things). I can see that fitting into EOE. Again, probably a leap, but...

Ever since I saw Federico Fellini's 1954 film La Strada , I've wondered if its finale served as inspiration for EOE's own conclusion.
